riddle:
Here is the revised riddle:

1. Seek the page about a Scottish boxer known for his achievements in the sport; note the fighting weight categories attributed to him who competed in the division above flyweight and below featherweight, and follow the small link labelled with the name given to that division to continue.

2. There exists an article about the division between flyweight and featherweight; on it, record the weight limit (in pounds) that applied at the time of the first gloved world-title fight.

3. What is the sum of the digits of the number you just discovered in clue 2? (Compute the digit-sum of that pound figure before moving on.)

4. If you consult the entry for the body that governs professional boxing in Britain, identify the month and year when its tribunal overturned the refusal to license Jane Couch.

5. The article that mentions the London club based in Covent Garden which promoted professional boxing and introduced the Lonsdale Belt invites a geographic check: count how many stadiums can be found near London.

6. The old ringkeeper asks you to finish with this arithmetic using only the numeric values you've gathered: compute abs(clue 2 - clue 3 + clue 5 - clue 5) % 10 and report the single digit result (0–9).

metadata.theme: Tracing a Scottish bantamweight's journey into the institutions and venues that shaped British boxing
